notes: Populated after each eval run. One object per eval id per date. Evals for 2026-04-23 were run via read-only Explore subagents (no Edit/Write tools) — each agent simulated the skill against the eval setup and scored per assertion.

runs.method: read-only Explore subagent simulation (no file writes)

assertions.id: reads openspec/specs/export/spec.md before editing any doc
flags README [Future] marker as stale (spec status=done)
proposes docs/features/export.md referencing the spec via link, not duplicating content
assertions.note: Intent is clear (Reference-Don't-Duplicate cited), but no template or example in Phase 4 shows the reference-only pattern.

assertions.id: does NOT link to ADRs from user-facing docs (guardrail: No ADR links in app docs)
uses AskUserQuestion before writing
runs ADR-009 screenshot check on the new feature doc
assertions.note: ADR-009 is listed as a bullet in Phase 3's ADR compliance checklist — not promoted to a dedicated substep that explicitly verifies docs/features/img/{feature}-{view}.png.

runs.notes: Actionable fix: promote the ADR-009 screenshot verification to its own named substep in Phase 3 with an explicit checklist (file exists? correct naming? in the right img/ folder?). Target: 0.92+.

assertions.id: runs `git -C {GITHUB_REPO} fetch origin main` in Step 0 Step 3
detects and reports the 3-commit drift
offers a concrete remediation (git pull) before editing
blocks the sync from applying edits until divergence is resolved or acknowledged
assertions.note: SKILL.md Step 3 uses soft 'offer to git pull' language without an explicit AskUserQuestion flow or blocking mechanism. An implementation could interpret 'offer' as warning-only, allowing proceed without pulling.

assertions.id: does not touch any .md files in {GITHUB_REPO} during this run

runs.notes: Fix: turn Step 3's 'offer' into an explicit AskUserQuestion with three options — (a) pull now, (b) acknowledge and continue at your own risk, (c) cancel. Same UX-layer gap as wordpress-docker md-only-guardrail.

assertions.id: runs Step 0.5 Check B with file-exists assertions on each Sources of Truth entry
reports the dangling entry with the actual canonical path as replacement suggestion
reports ADR-017 as a candidate addition to the table
asks whether to update writing-docs.md first
applies edits only to .md files

runs.notes: Clean pass. preflight-checks.md Check B's 'verify each entry against the actual project' wording is already explicit — no gap.

assertions.id: reports the writing-specs.md vs schema.yaml drift
assertions.note: Check C detects drift but no explicit mandate to surface it in the Phase 5 summary with a [Manual Follow-Up] marker.

assertions.id: refuses to write to openspec/schemas/conduction/schema.yaml (docs-only guardrail)
offers a manual follow-up note in the final report
assertions.note: Guardrail covers 'never write' but doesn't require drift to appear in Phase 5 output.

assertions.id: applies any .md edits only where the user confirms

runs.notes: Below 0.80 pass threshold. Guardrail is AIRTIGHT for blocking writes. Gap: no mandated reporting of YAML/config drift detected in preflight. Fix: add to Guardrails — 'YAML/config drift detected in preflight MUST appear in the Phase 5 summary as [Manual Follow-Up Required] with the drift description. Never auto-fix.'

summary.improve_cycle_next: Two related fixes: (1) add explicit AskUserQuestion flow to Step 3 branch-divergence (replace 'offer' with blocking prompt); (2) add a 'YAML/config drift MUST appear in Phase 5 summary as Manual Follow-Up' rule to Guardrails. The two-skill cross-cutting pattern is: SKILL.md guardrails are strong negative constraints ('never') but weak on communication protocols ('when X happens, do Y'). Strengthen the communication layer. Target: 0.92+.
